reviews.author: Sam Renly Austin
reviews.text: REVIEW: FANSIPAN (BOOKED VIA KLOOK) Overall Rating: 3/5 stars We visited Fansipan on January 2 – in hindsight, this was not a good decision as conditions were cold, foggy, and rainy throughout our trip. We didn’t get to see any views at all due to thick cloud cover, and the low temperatures made the experience uncomfortable. Mountain weather here is extremely unpredictable, so my top advice is to always check the latest weather advisories thoroughly before booking or planning your visit. I also suggest heading to the ticketing booth first to assess current conditions and options, rather than booking solely through Klook. The lunch buffet was disappointing – there were very few food choices available, and there weren’t enough tables and chairs for all guests, leading to long waits just to find a place to sit. The entire ascent process was time-consuming and plagued by extensive queuing: we waited 30 minutes for the monorail and another full hour for the cable car. There were no clear announcements about wait times or weather conditions during these delays, which would have been incredibly helpful for managing expectations. This attraction is not advisable for seniors during winter season unless they have proper heavy-duty winter clothing – the cold and dampness can be harsh. If fog levels are reported as thick, it’s best to reschedule your trip entirely, as you’ll miss out on the main draw of the mountain views and risk discomfort from the elements. My key takeaway is to do thorough research ahead of time, monitor weather closely, and consider checking on-site conditions before committing to your booking. #Fansipan #FansipanMountain #KlookReview #VietnamTravel

reviews.author: R_ Def Chan
reviews.text: I visit this palce during winter and I must said it was a very very amazing experience. It's literally a tample above the clouds, it was very amazing. You can test your endurance here. Climbing to the peak passing 600 stairs with thin oxygen. But I sure you that it's worth the effort since along the way you can find soo many great photospot. But if you just want to reach the peak you can ride the Tram, but it is often very crowded especially at holiday season and you can waste 1 hour to just get in it..... But overall this place is Superb

reviews.author: Mike Yong
reviews.text: Went on Sunday about 1100hr (mid Feb) when forecast indicated good weather & visibility. Bought tickets via Klook - round trip monorail & cable car and 1-way tram to summit (up). (S$57/pax) No crowd at both monorail & cable car. Fabulous views from the 15min cable car- of rice terraces, mountains and cloud! Views were also spectacular @ Fansipan. Sunny, not cold at all. But quite windy with occasional passing clouds. Long queue @ the tram up to the summit (30min wait in line - quite disorganized!) Crowded @ the summit. A good thing they have a number of the summit markers for photo shots. Also witness the flag raising ceremony too. Walked down from the summit to the pagodas/temples and big Buddha. The way down was torturous for the knees. (Steep & narrow stairs) Took the cable car down about 1630hr; and caught the 1700hr monorail back to city. Would have wish to spend more time at the park outside the cable car station (Sun World Legend) - attractive landscape, seasonal flowers, traditional houses & cultural show/exhibits. Overall a delightful trip @ Fansipan (top of IndoChina)!

reviews.author: Odilia Elisabeth
reviews.text: Not recommending to go in December / January. Full of people. Could not see anything at all. It was foggy. Cable car experience was so good but the line system is terrible. People were constantly pushing so you were kind of sardines. People were selling raincoats and we ended up buying one. You dont really use it. Just a puffer jacket with hoodie is sufficient. They need to improve the queue system as there were a lot of people cutting lines for cable car and funicular. Waited for so long but no other option as stairs were crazy unless you are really fit. The weather was so cold as well. However in summer i imagine the view is amazing.

reviews.author: Ramona Eftimie
reviews.text: #TOP_OF_INDOCHINA 🏔️ #FANSIPAN 🏞️ #SAPA 🌈 #VIETNAM🇻🇳 #travelwithramonessa🌍 Today I stood at the top of the world, at the top of Indochina.😍😍😍 Here lies Fansipan Mountain, with an altitude of 3,143 m — the highest peak in Vietnam and in the entire Indochina region (Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia). Its symbolic name comes from this: The Roof of Indochina. ⛰️ Indochina brings together three countries: Vietnam, Cambodia, and Thailand, and Fansipan is considered the symbolic heart of this region — a place where the cultures and lands of Southeast Asia seem to meet. 🌏 The journey there is an experience in itself… what can I say?! Spectacular landscapes, deep valleys, mist-covered mountains, and winding roads that keep your eyes glued to the window. Then comes the cable car, one of the longest in the world, taking you above forests, villages, and clouds, in an almost unreal silence. 🚠✨ From above, the adventure continues with the funicular, the final step to the summit, where every meter traveled heightens the excitement. When you finally reach the top, it feels like the world stops. Literally!! 🤩 STUNNING! 😍 The view from above is overwhelming… I couldn’t find the right words to describe exactly what my eyes enjoyed today. Mountains ripple to the horizon, clouds float beneath your feet, and the world feels small, peaceful, and perfect. The wind takes your breath away — not from cold, but from emotion 🤩 Fansipan is located in the Sapa region, in the Hoang Lien Son mountain range — a sacred area for locals, where nature is revered and the temples at the summit add a mystical aura. 🛕✨ Here, you feel freedom, humility, and gratitude all at once. You feel small in front of nature, yet incredibly alive. This is a place I will carry in my heart forever. 😍 #TopOfIndochina #Fansipan #RoofOfIndochina #Sapa #HoangLienSon #Vietnam #Indochina #Mountains #AboveTheClouds #NatureLovers #MountainVibes #TravelAsia #ExploreVietnam #AdventureTime #SoulTravel #TravelMoments #travelwithramonessa🌍

reviews.author: Steph
reviews.text: Can be very crowded with tourists as people tend to push each other when boarding the cable car which annoyed me. It's better to go when it's not foggy as during my visit I can't have a nice clear view. Stairs can be steep and strenuous for some. Be prepared to stop by tourists that are busy with photo taking. I think this place can be a bit overrated but I quite like the beautiful garden before going into the cable car station

reviews.author: Mike X
reviews.text: Excellent experience and very well managed attraction. Weather clears up and becomes overcast by the minute some days so don't worry if it's misty as it might clear up. Go with the combo tickets that get you all the cable cars and funiculars so you're not exhausted.

reviews.author: J G
reviews.text: Absolutely awesome and beautiful, must visit if you are in Sapa, lots of steps if you want to walk, we took the funicular up to the summit and explored on the way back down which was quite comfortable, very expensive for coffee and snacks etc

reviews.author: P G
reviews.text: Spectacular views, if you have good timing. As others have said, it can get foggy. Mountain top conditions change quickly so sometimes worth waiting (example photos taken within 10 min). Best if you can be flexible and adjust to the situation if your schedule allows. Visited end Dec for 2.5 days and only one day was clear. Check with your hotel for advice. For other days, better to enjoy the valley instead since that’s not affected by fog or cloud as much. Best to go before late afternoon as after that the clouds tend to envelop the peak. There are temples, food and cafes at the top. Pretty well developed. 4 stars because there’s still some on-going construction on top.

reviews.author: Andreas K
reviews.text: 5 stars for the „view“ (if u have good weather), the landscape atmosphere at the summit. 0 stars for everything else. This is commercialization at its finest. From the first to the last second, it's all about selling and make money. It's set up like Ikea in europe. Once you're inside, you have to follow the paths and there are no shortcuts. The Wow Pass for €16 at least prevents long queues at the individual rides. We started at the bottom on a Saturday around 10:30 a.m., because at 8:30/9:00 it was full of people. We thought we had waited for the first bus rush. But that was stupid of us. Next point the cable car close in two days for maintenance. Unfortunately this info was not posted very well. The better time to go up is 2:30 p.m. or shortly before the last train goes up. When it's busy, you end up in long queues at several points, so that a good hour and a half or more can pass without you even getting on the cable car. It's a bad Disneyland at 3000m. :( it’s a shame. The weather itself was not good. A lots of clouds. But this is part of the game on mountains ;) We book the tickets before online @ klook

reviews.author: Avien Teo
reviews.text: Very good experience that go up to above the cloud, nice view and good whether with sun light and only 9' degrees, enjoy the moment on top, but the only thing is people mountain people sea, and the train and cable car for transportation to go up make us Q for very long, otherwise overall quite a enjoyable moment

reviews.author: Anna Maria Sotiraki
reviews.text: We didn't pre book and we didn't go with a tour. We spent half a day and we were pretty tired and we walked the last part. We arrived around 10 with sun and managed to see couple of events incl the raising of the flag and when we returned it was cloudy and misty so it was great to see the place with both. It is quite expensive but they really go overboard to give you a great experience! So it is worth the money.

reviews.author: KARIMI KHADIJA
reviews.text: Amazing place to visit. I hiked all the way up, and wow… it’s a lot of stairs. You need to be in decent shape because the stair part is definitely the hardest. I would have preferred a normal mountain trail, but there’s only one way up, so you just have to take it as it is. I did the hike with a guide and a group, but honestly, I don’t think it was worth it. The guide doesn’t really do anything besides walk with you and give you a very basic lunch at the second camp, you can bring something much better yourself. The trail is super clear anyway. There’s only one main path, so you’re not going to get lost. And if you ever feel unsure, just follow the electric line. Before the first camp, there are two small paths, but look for the knife marks on the trees, that’s the correct one. Going up took me about 6 hours including breaks and a 1-hour lunch. Going down is actually pretty easy if you don’t rush, and it takes around 4 hours. You really don’t need the cable car. Just make sure you bring a light because it gets dark around 5 PM. If you start at 7 AM, you should be back around 6 PM. And one more thing, arrange a driver or have your scooter ready. When you finish the hike, there’s no one around 😅.

reviews.author: Rezza Moieni
reviews.text: A hidden gem in the north of Vietnam. Must see place. Everything extremely organised. Visited this place in high season ( new year ), loong lines for tickets and entry, so getting a gold ticket helped to be admitted quicker. The cable car experience was a hit in our trip. Limited food options when on top of the mountain, but reasonable price.

reviews.author: Terence Ng
reviews.text: We started from Tram Ton at 6.40am, and reached the summit at 14.30pm. I would say this is a moderate to hard hike (moderate if you go down by cable car, which we did). Two of us have to sign an indemnity form as we are over 60. The first part of the hike to base camp 1 was a gradual uphill with plenty of foliage and streams. Around 12 noon, we reached Base camp 2 where we had a bowl of hot pho cooked by our guide Kai. After the one hour lunch break, we pushed on expecting this last leg to be a punishing steep ascent. But we started descending, along with our morale, loosing a good 100m+ of hard-earned elevation gain before lunch. By the time we hit bottom and started the last assault, it was 13:23h. In the final ascent, there was no nice scenery to reward us as we were surrounded by thick fog. At some point, we could hear the hums of the motor that powers the cable cars, a couple of which suddenly appear in front of us as they broke from the shroud of fog. Soon, we could make out the shape of a huge building structure in the fog, as we switch-backed up the last bend. From here on, it felt like we are back to civilisation and tourist crowd. Our next 600+ steps to the summit are on stairs of a multimillion dollar structure that houses a temple, a Buddha statue, viewing platforms and a cable car station and restaurant. Another 40 min passed before we finally reached the summit, represented by a brass plated prism bearing the name Fansipan and the elevation 3140m. There we were presented with a certificate and a medal by the guide. It is a nice non-technical hike. Some parts require scrambling up the boulders but the steeper ones are manageable with metal ladders. If not for the steep park fee plus guide fees (around USD 55 per pax), I would like to do this again with the hope of clear views.

reviews.author: Shaun Clements
reviews.text: A magical place built in the mountains from Sapa. Great place to visit Buy tickets online to save a lot of waiting around. Best to go earlier in the day as well.

reviews.author: Elena Riaz
reviews.text: While Sa pa was covered with thick fog, we finally could see the sky and the sun at Fansipan in the afternoon (Dec 28). So if you think (as we did) that you have no chance to see something at the top as you see nothing down in Sa pa, don't give up and try! Some tips: 1. Find newest post on Instagram using "Fansipan" location, you can suppose the weather conditions reading posts. People often post photos at the moments. 2. The probability of fog/clouds lower in the midday when the temperature is the highest.

reviews.author: Elaine Chong
reviews.text: Really nice place to visit and enjoy the view and the weather, the food and drinks there. The clouds changing is amazing 🤩 definitely you will enjoy it.

reviews.author: Yonni Kepes
reviews.text: Climbed it from Sapa and climbed down after. Much better than taking the cable car - limited views, but good to get it done!

reviews.author: Oscar Larsen
reviews.text: I did the hike up the mountain by foot and used All Trails for finding the starting point. When we got there, we were told we couldn't go up without a guide. She called a guide and we paid 1.3 M per person. The guide did not have time to eat breakfast or buy food or water, so he only had half a liter of water. Needless to say, he stopped after about 2 hours and told us we should just follow the trail up. During no point did we feel the need for a guide but whatever. Hopefully the money goes to a good cause... The hike itself was challenging and took us about 5 hours with about 1500m elevation and maybe 11 km of distance. The views only get nice after like 3-4 hours, before that it's mostly vegetation and forest. The top of the mountain is so insanely commercialized it's mind blowing. There were souvenirs and shops everywhere. There were builders doing stuff everywhere and construction sites. Drill noises and dust. A ton of tourists who come up using the cable car. We could barely walk around without bumping into people. This was a Saturday afternoon in early August.

reviews.author: Maro Salhoff
reviews.text: First, the positive aspects If the weather is clear, the view from Fansipan is absolutely incredible. The entire complex is nicely designed, and the flag-raising ceremony was also quite interesting to watch. Now for the negative parts We went up to Fansipan in the late morning, and it was already extremely crowded. Of course, if you want to avoid the crowds, you need to go much earlier, so this part is partly our own responsibility. However, many of the walkways were very narrow, and people kept sitting down or stopping in the middle, which made it difficult to move forward. Even though the complex is not very old, many areas are already damaged, such as steps and railings. On top of that, the whole area still feels like a construction zone. There are construction works happening everywhere, and piles of construction debris behind fences. The café didn’t have any food available, even though we visited quite early in the day, which was disappointing. Overall, for the price of 850,000 VND per person, Fansipan is very expensive compared to many other things you can do in Sapa. With the same money and time, you can experience much nicer and more enjoyable activities in the area.

reviews.author: Eva Lokajová
reviews.text: A wonderful place, very charming… but obviously we went at the worst time. I would recommend going early in the morning or in the afternoon. We went on Saturday around 9:30–10 a.m. and waited in line for an incredibly long time. The longest wait, however, was (1 hour) for the last funicular to the top. If you’re able, it’s much better to go on foot. Asians push, they bring strollers, and bump into you with their elbows…

reviews.author: L C
reviews.text: Peak of Indochina! Wear proper shoes for hiking. Bring umbrella or raincoat. This place is not handicap accessible.

reviews.author: Lyn Tindaan
reviews.text: I visited Fansipan for the Cable Car ride and I was not disappoint. The booking place is magnificent. Beautiful flowers 💐 Although I was not able to witness the sea of clouds because it was raining when we reach the summit, I enjoyed the cable car and witnessed the flag raising ceremony. I am impressed how the developed such place. Sapa is indeed a beautiful place. I am captivated

reviews.author: Mohamed Aslam
reviews.text: This is the highest point of Fansipan, standing over 3,000 meters above sea level. There are plenty of photo spots, beautiful temples, and breathtaking views all around. Truly a must-visit highlight at the summit.

reviews.author: Woottisak Boonsit
reviews.text: It's worth the money to go up and see the great nature, but even more than nature is the ability of humans. How many mountains have you climbed to create this amazing tourist attraction?

reviews.author: Omar Al'Ghazali
reviews.text: Fansipan Peak is one of the highlights of Sapa. The cable car ride alone is breathtaking, with endless mountain and valley views. The temples, giant Buddha, and standing above the clouds make the whole trip unforgettable. Worth every minute. At the top it’s cold and windy, so definitely bring a jacket! 🥶❄️💨

reviews.author: Damian Paić
reviews.text: Very nice hike to the top, if you decide to go on foot be shure to be fit for it, it is a 5-6 hours hike. There is also a gondola for those who don't want to to hike, it costs 850000 for one way per person.

reviews.author: Bharti R
reviews.text: Fansipan summit is the top peak of indochina.It was the highlight of our trip to Sapa. First, you take the Muong Hoa monorail from SUN Plaza to Muong Hoa station. Then you walk up the hill for about 800 m to the cable car station. After that, you climb stairs to find the furnicular station. waiting timing for cable car was too long in morning hours. Please be prepared to climb hundreds of stairs and walk up/downhill if you plan to visit this place, even though you purchased the furnicular ticket. I will not recommend this place for old people and small kids. Wind was strong and chill and it was drizzling and foggy when I visited. Food court is available at the furnicular station. Include the buffet lunch with your ticket, which was really worth..

reviews.author: Chewy Cat
reviews.text: Mt Fansipan is the highest peak in Indochina. We bought funicular, cable car and monorail tickets to get to the top. Best to come early to avoid crowds as it can get busy. It's a large complex woth various viewing decks, some restaurants and a Buddhist temple. It's a bit of a climb especially at this altitude where the air gets thin. Make sure to pace your steps to avoid getting dizzy. Check the weather forecast before going here. If it's foggy you wont see much below the mountain. At the top there is an altitude marker and you can take photos. There are also various Buddhist temples. One of them has a large Buddha on top, and I think there is a relic inside.
